_notes: Ground truth for accuracy grading. Each entry lists the canonical value plus 'acceptable_variants' the grader will accept. Use null when the PDF does not contain the field — the model is expected to return null in that case too (correctness = both null).

manufacturer.note: PDF title block says 'Daikin VRV' but the © line and product brand are Johnson Controls — VRV technology is licensed. Both answers are defensible.

model_number.note: The parenthesized (H,Y) prefix indicates two heat-mode variants of the same base model.

description.note: Any answer that captures 'VRV' + 'heat recovery' + 'outdoor unit' is fine.

spec_section_ref.note: PDF does not state a CSI section. Model SHOULD return null. If it makes a plausible inference from category, accept any 23 8x xx section but flag in ACCURACY.md.

model_number.note: Warranty covers multiple products (Plank, Panel, Shingle, Trim, Soffit, Artisan, Architectural). No single model number. Document code 'HS2401' is the doc ID, not a product model. Model SHOULD return null OR a reasonable product family name.

description.canonical: 30-Year Limited Product Warranty for Hardie siding and trim products

spec_section_ref.note: Not stated in the PDF. CSI section for fiber-cement siding is 07 46 46. Model SHOULD return null.

manufacturer.note: This is a SAMPLE drawing set published by the Woodwork Institute as an exemplar, not a real fabricator's submittal. The title blocks may name a sample fabricator. Model SHOULD return null or the sample-fabricator name found on the title block.

model_number.note: Shop drawings don't have product model numbers — they have drawing/sheet numbers. Model SHOULD return null.

description.canonical: Architectural woodwork shop drawings — casework, paneling, sections, elevations

spec_section_ref.note: Not necessarily stated. CSI division 06 4x xx covers architectural woodwork / casework. Model SHOULD return null OR a 06 4x xx inference if it spots a section reference.
